title: campanula mollis subsp. winkleri
intro: Campanula mollis subsp. winkleri, also known as the Winkler's campanula, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae), characterized by its delicate purple flowers and soft, hairy leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce swelling, and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Campanula mollis subsp. winkleri include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is often used to treat colds, flu, and other infections.
